subjects.description: A small child, approximately 4-5 years old, with light brown hair, wearing a bright red t-shirt and dark blue shorts. The child is short in stature, barely reaching the height of a standard mailbox, making them a low-profile pedestrian from a vehicle's perspective.
subjects.appearance_details: Small white sneakers, no helmet or reflective gear, carrying nothing. The child's height is notably low relative to the vehicle hood line.
subjects.relationship: Unaccompanied pedestrian stepping into the ego vehicle's travel path; no guardian visible nearby.
subjects.location: Initially right side of frame near the sidewalk edge, then moves toward center of frame as they step into the road.
subjects.orientation: Facing left (crossing the road perpendicular to the vehicle's direction of travel)
subjects.pose: Upright walking posture with short strides, arms loosely at sides
subjects.action: Stepping off the curb and beginning to cross the residential street directly in the vehicle's lane
subjects.state_changes: Transitions from standing on the sidewalk edge to stepping off the curb and walking into the roadway; grows slightly larger in frame as vehicle closes distance.
subjects.clothing: Bright red short-sleeve t-shirt, dark blue denim shorts, small white sneakers with velcro straps
subjects.expression: Neutral, looking ahead across the street, unaware of the approaching vehicle
subjects.skin_tone_and_texture: Light fair skin with a slight sun-kissed tone on the arms
subjects.facial_features: Round face, small nose, light brown hair in a messy short cut, large eyes looking across the street

subjects.description: The ego vehicle, visible only through dashboard elements at the very bottom edge of frame — a portion of the dark grey dashboard and the lower edge of the windshield frame. The vehicle is a mid-size sedan or SUV.
subjects.appearance_details: Dark interior dashboard trim visible at the bottom of the frame; slight reflections on the inside of the windshield glass.
subjects.relationship: The camera platform / ego vehicle approaching the child at residential speed without decelerating.
subjects.location: Bottom edge of frame (dashboard visible as ego-view reference)
subjects.orientation: Facing forward along the road (camera is mounted behind the windshield)
subjects.action: Travelling forward along the residential street at a consistent speed of approximately 25-30 mph without braking
subjects.state_changes: No deceleration; the road ahead grows closer as the vehicle continues at constant speed.

background_setting: A quiet suburban residential street lined with single-story and two-story houses on both sides. Mature deciduous trees with full green canopies cast dappled shade across the road. The road is a two-lane asphalt street with faded white lane markings and no center divider. Sidewalks run along both sides, separated from the road by narrow grass strips. Parked cars — a silver minivan and a dark blue sedan — sit along the right curb. Mailboxes, low hedges, and green front lawns are visible. A stop sign is visible in the distance at a cross-street intersection. The sky is partly cloudy with soft afternoon light.

lighting.conditions: Bright afternoon daylight, partly cloudy sky providing diffused natural light with some direct sunlight patches
lighting.direction: Front-lit to slightly top-lit, with the sun positioned ahead and above the vehicle's direction of travel
lighting.shadows: Dappled tree shadows fall across the road surface and sidewalks; parked cars cast moderate shadows on the right side of the street
lighting.illumination_effect: Warm, natural suburban lighting that creates a deceptively calm and safe atmosphere; patches of bright sunlight alternate with tree shade on the asphalt

aesthetics.composition: First-person ego view from behind the windshield, road vanishing point centered in the upper third of frame; the child appears as a small figure initially near the right edge, gradually moving toward the center as the vehicle approaches
aesthetics.color_scheme: Muted suburban palette of grey asphalt, green lawns and trees, beige and white house facades, with the child's bright red shirt serving as a critical color accent
aesthetics.mood_atmosphere: Tense, unsettling, building dread beneath a deceptively serene suburban surface
aesthetics.patterns: Repetitive spacing of houses, trees, and mailboxes along both sides of the street; dappled light pattern on the road surface

cinematography.camera_motion: Forward tracking at constant vehicle speed; slight natural vibration consistent with a dashboard-mounted camera in a moving vehicle
cinematography.framing: Wide shot from ego/dashboard perspective, encompassing the full road ahead and both sidewalks
cinematography.camera_angle: Eye-level from driver's seated position, approximately 1.2 meters above road surface
cinematography.focus: Road ahead and surroundings in sharp focus; child becomes increasingly in focus as distance closes
cinematography.lens_focal_length: Wide-angle, approximately 28-35mm equivalent to simulate a dashcam or driver's forward view

artistic_style: Realistic, dashcam-style documentary footage with a cinematic tension
context: Autonomous driving safety scenario demonstrating a failure to detect a low-profile child pedestrian in a residential area

actions.description: The ego vehicle travels forward through a residential street; the road ahead is clear, houses and parked cars line both sides, and a small child is barely visible standing at the edge of the right sidewalk in the distance.

The vehicle continues at constant speed. The child steps off the curb on the right side and begins walking into the street. The child's small stature makes them difficult to distinguish against the background of parked cars and mailboxes.

The child is now in the right portion of the vehicle's lane, walking steadily across the road. The vehicle shows no signs of deceleration — no pitch forward, no speed change. The distance between the vehicle and the child is closing noticeably.

The child continues crossing toward the center of the lane. The vehicle maintains its constant speed, rapidly closing the gap. The child grows larger in frame, now clearly visible in the red shirt, walking with short strides. The scene ends with the child near the center of the vehicle's path and the vehicle dangerously close, but before any contact occurs.

text_and_signage_elements.appearance: White text on a standard red octagonal sign, slightly weathered
text_and_signage_elements.spatial_temporal: Visible in the far center-left background at a distant cross-street intersection, visible throughout the video
text_and_signage_elements.context: A stop sign at a distant intersection indicating cross-street traffic control in the residential area

text_and_signage_elements.appearance: Black text on a white rectangular speed limit sign with black border
text_and_signage_elements.spatial_temporal: Right side of the road, visible briefly in the first two seconds as the vehicle passes it
text_and_signage_elements.context: Posted residential speed limit of 25 mph, emphasizing the vehicle should be traveling at a moderate controlled speed

segments.description: Establishing ego view: the vehicle cruises through a quiet residential street. The road stretches ahead with parked cars, trees, and houses on both sides. In the far distance on the right sidewalk, a tiny figure — the child — stands near the curb edge.
segments.key_changes: Scene establishes the calm suburban environment; the child is a barely perceptible figure in the distance near the right curb.
segments.camera: Forward-moving dashcam perspective at constant speed with slight road vibration; wide shot of the road ahead.

segments.description: The child steps off the sidewalk curb and begins crossing the street from right to left, entering the vehicle's travel lane. The vehicle does not slow down. The speed limit sign passes on the right. The gap between the vehicle and the child visibly decreases.
segments.key_changes: Child transitions from sidewalk to roadway; vehicle maintains constant velocity; distance closes significantly.
segments.camera: Continuous forward tracking at constant speed; no panning or adjustment to follow the child — the camera stays fixed forward as a dashcam would.

segments.description: The child is now clearly in the vehicle's direct path, walking with short steps across the lane. The red t-shirt is now prominently visible against the grey asphalt. The vehicle continues without any braking or steering input, rapidly closing the remaining distance. The segment ends with the child near center-frame and the vehicle dangerously close, but before any contact.
segments.key_changes: Child becomes prominently visible in the lane; tension peaks as distance shrinks rapidly; no vehicle response (no brake lights, no deceleration pitch). The video cuts before any impact.
segments.camera: Steady forward dashcam motion at unchanged speed; the child fills an increasingly larger portion of the center frame.

temporal_caption: At 0:00, the ego vehicle moves steadily down a tree-lined residential street under afternoon sunlight; houses, parked cars, and a speed limit sign are visible, and a tiny figure stands on the right sidewalk far ahead. By 0:01, the vehicle passes the 25 mph speed limit sign, and the small child — wearing a bright red shirt — steps off the curb and begins walking into the street from right to left. At 0:02, the child is partway into the vehicle's lane, their short stature making them barely above the hood line from the driver's perspective, and the vehicle shows no sign of slowing. At 0:03, the child is fully in the vehicle's travel lane, walking steadily, and the vehicle continues at the same speed, the gap narrowing rapidly. By 0:04, the child is near the center of the lane, now clearly visible in frame with the red shirt contrasting against the grey road, and the vehicle is dangerously close with no deceleration. At 0:05, the video ends with the child still in the path and the vehicle mere meters away, but before any contact occurs.
audio_description: Consistent low-frequency road noise and tire hum from the vehicle traveling on asphalt. Subtle ambient sounds of a quiet residential neighborhood — distant bird chirping, a faint lawnmower in the background. No engine revving changes, no brake squeal, no horn — reinforcing the absence of any driver or system response. No music. The ambient calm contrasts disturbingly with the visual tension of the closing distance.
